fixes_applied: Replaced tests/test.sh: was the empty-FAIL_TO_PASS SWE-rebench template that ran `bun test 2>&1` and fell back to overall pass rate (0.878-0.909 reward across deepseek-v4-pro, deepseek-v4-flash, opus-4.6, glm-5.1, minimax-m2.7 trials — confirmed scoring trap inflating no-effort runs into the ceiling). New test.sh runs 5 inline F2P gates against /opt/rudel: 4 chart structural checks (via tests/check_ast.py) plus 1 biome lint check; writes per-gate verdicts to /logs/verifier/gates.json and emits a weighted-replace reward.

Rewrote tests/check_ast.py: the prior JSX-tokenizer-based implementation could not parse ProjectDetailPage.tsx because the file mixes TypeScript generics (e.g., `useMemo[]>`) with JSX, which the naive `` heading, extracts the smallest enclosing `...` block via balanced scan, then runs scoped regex on Legend/XAxis/margin props.

Rewrote tests/test_manifest.yaml: dropped the stale `nav_fallback` F2P gate (was for ProjectsListPage handleRowClick fallback — Turn 1 of the user simulator does NOT direct the agent to this file, so the gate was misaligned with the simulator's intervention pattern); dropped the `types_pass` F2P gate (apps/web `tsc -b` exits with code 2 against base commit 6222e1d7 due to 462 pre-existing TS errors in unrelated files: AdminPage admin namespace, posthog-js / html-to-image / @testing-library/react missing imports, product-analytics namespace exports — gating on tsc would always FAIL in both buggy and fixed states); added a positive `xaxis_truncation` F2P gate that requires tickFormatter / custom tick prop / vertical-layout overlap mitigation (positive evidence of the user's overlap fix, complementing the negative xaxis_fixed gate). Final F2P weight sum = 1.00 across 5 gates.

Set _upgraded_from_swerebench_template: true and _test_sh_uses_manifest: true in tests/install_config.json to mark this as a manifest-driven verifier; the SWE-rebench fields FAIL_TO_PASS/PASS_TO_PASS remain empty arrays — they're not used by the new test.sh which scores via gates.json instead.
f2p_added: legend_right (weight 0.30) — Legend in Contributors BarChart uses vertical right-side layout (accepts either explicit layout="vertical" align="right" props OR ChartLegend content renderer)
xaxis_fixed (weight 0.25) — XAxis no longer uses angle={-45} or textAnchor="end" (negative check)
xaxis_truncation (weight 0.15) — XAxis applies a tick-overlap mitigation: tickFormatter, custom tick prop, or vertical chart layout (positive check)
margin_standard (weight 0.15) — BarChart margin no longer uses { bottom: 60 } (any standardized margin or default accepted)
lint_pass (weight 0.15) — biome check passes on the 3 source files the canonical commit touches; Turn 5 of the user-sim explicitly reports a CI biome import-ordering failure
notes:
Triple-check overturns DROP. Original DROP rationale ("UI-only chart UX with no test infra... scoring degenerates to 'unrelated bun test pass rate'") was correct in diagnosing the SWE-rebench template trap (0.878-0.909 across all v0.4.3 cohorts confirmed it), but underweighted that (a) chart UX changes ARE testable structurally per the rescue context, (b) the task already had a partial test_manifest.yaml + check_ast.py — the gates were declared but never executed, and (c) the canonical commit f5bc932 is small and well-defined (3 files, 23 additions, 10 deletions).

RESCUE PATHS CONSIDERED:

1. Restore legacy.bak (cc-backend / amytis-implement-3b6cb5 path) — N/A. No tests/test.sh.legacy.bak exists for this task. The current test.sh is the SWE-rebench template that ships with build_swerebench_configs.py.

2. Patch contains tests we missed — N/A. Canonical patch f5bc932 contains zero test files (3 source files only). The rudel repo has unit tests under apps/api/__tests__/ but none cover web/recharts components.

3. Write fresh structural gates (amytis-task-103a94 path) — YES. The check_ast.py + test_manifest.yaml in tests/ already declared the right gate IDs (legend_right, xaxis_fixed, margin_standard, chart_section_exists) but had two latent bugs: (a) the JSX tokenizer in check_ast.py was broken on TS-generic-heavy files (couldn't parse ProjectDetailPage.tsx at all), (b) the test.sh was the SWE-rebench template that ignored the manifest entirely. Both were fixable: rewrote check_ast.py with anchor-based block extraction + scoped regex, rewrote test.sh to run each gate's command and aggregate via weighted-replace.

4. Strip patch noise — YES, partial. The artifacts_unknown/canonical_patches/6c4daa34*.json ("reconstructed from session messages", _fidelity: "exact") records 4 files including 3 chart components (DeveloperTrendChart, DimensionAnalysisChart, ProjectTrendChart) with substantial sortedDevelopers/sortedProjects/splitTotals sorting logic. These are ABSENT from the actual canonical commit f5bc93207485e39d7782354d063d72f3809b17cd, which only touches 3 files. The user-simulation prompt confirms why: Turn 3 says 'Something weird is happening. When I change metrics in these charts the color changes... Let's revert this change completely.' The tool_replay reconstruction captured the agent's mid-session sort attempt that the user later reverted. I aligned the gates to the actual commit (Contributors chart UX in ProjectDetailPage.tsx + biome lint), not the stale reconstruction. The 3 chart files were correctly stripped from gate coverage — adding gates for sortedDevelopers/etc. would penalize the ground-truth final state.

5. Drop nav_fallback — YES. The prior manifest declared a `nav_fallback` F2P gate (weight 0.15) for ProjectsListPage handleRowClick fallback. Although the canonical commit DOES touch ProjectsListPage.tsx (adds row.repository to the navigation key fallback), the user-simulator's Turn 1 instruction is solely about the Contributors chart UX. Turns 2-3 are the chart-sort fix-then-revert; Turn 4 is 'commit and update PR'; Turn 5 is the biome lint CI fix. None of the user turns direct the agent to ProjectsListPage. Including nav_fallback would punish agents who correctly followed the user's directive — measuring 'agent picked up extra work the user didn't ask for' rather than 'agent answered the user's question'. Dropped.

6. Drop types_pass — YES. The apps/web `tsc -b` exits with code 2 against base commit 6222e1d7 (462 lines of TS errors in unrelated files: AdminPage admin namespace, posthog-js / html-to-image / @testing-library/react missing imports). Including types_pass would always FAIL in both buggy and fixed states, providing zero signal and confusing operators reading gates.json. Replaced with `xaxis_truncation` to keep weight sum at 1.00.

SMOKING-GUN VALIDATION PERFORMED: cloned obsessiondb/rudel @ 6222e1d7f08abe42654d766092d7bcd26177a446 to /tmp/rudel_check, ran the new check_ast.py against (a) buggy baseline checked out at 6222e1d7 and (b) canonical-fix state checked out at f5bc93207485e39d7782354d063d72f3809b17cd. Also installed dependencies (`bun install` produced @biomejs/biome@2.4.2) and ran the lint_pass gate command in both states.

Baseline (buggy, no agent work):
  legend_right       -> FAIL (Legend has neither vertical+right nor ChartLegend)
  xaxis_fixed        -> FAIL (XAxis still uses angle={-45})
  xaxis_truncation   -> FAIL (no tickFormatter/tick/vertical)
  margin_standard    -> FAIL (BarChart margin={{ bottom: 60 }})
  lint_pass          -> PASS (base commit's 3 files are biome-clean)
  chart_section_exists -> PASS (informational, doesn't affect reward)
  Verdict: 1/5 F2P pass -> reward = 0.15 (lint_pass weight only)

Canonical-fix (f5bc932 applied):
  legend_right       -> PASS
  xaxis_fixed        -> PASS
  xaxis_truncation   -> PASS (tickFormatter on XAxis truncates to 12 chars)
  margin_standard    -> PASS
  lint_pass          -> PASS
  chart_section_exists -> PASS
  Verdict: 5/5 F2P pass -> reward = 1.00

The 0.15 -> 1.00 spread is sharp and well-calibrated: a no-op agent gets 0.15 (the lint floor, since unchanged files are biome-clean), a complete fix gets 1.00, and partial credit is smooth (e.g., legend-only fix = 0.15 + 0.30 = 0.45). The previous test.sh would have given a no-op agent ~0.91 (the inflated bun-test pass rate that scored 0.878-0.909 across all 5 v0.4.3 cohorts).

WHY THIS IS A KEEPER: 5 specific behavioral checks, each implementation-agnostic on the public surface (Legend props, XAxis props, BarChart margin pattern, biome lint exit code). Multi-turn signal is real: Turn 1 directs the chart fix, Turn 5 directs the lint fix, both directly testable. Reward formula is standard weighted-replace per CLAUDE.md.

KNOWN RISKS:
- check_ast.py uses regex (not a full TS+JSX parser); it could miss exotic formatting (e.g., Legend split across nested function components). The anchor-based block extraction tolerates Prettier/Biome formatting variations and the gate tests verified-pass on the canonical commit. If a future agent uses a wildly different idiom (e.g., a custom <RightLegend /> component without the layout/align props or ChartLegend), the gate will conservatively FAIL — the ChartLegend acceptance covers the most common idiomatic alternative.
- The F2P weight sum is exactly 1.00 (replace formula), so the ceiling is 1.00 with no inner-reward residual. A 1.00 reward requires all 5 gates pass — no partial-credit ceiling for honest-but-incomplete fixes. Smooth gradient (0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.55, 0.70, 0.85, 1.00) provides discrimination.

baseline_buggy.note: lint_pass passes because base commit's 3 files are biome-clean; chart_section_exists is informational P2P

v043_trap_evidence.previous_test_sh: ran `bun test 2>&1` with empty FAIL_TO_PASS, parsed via parse_log_bun_test, fell back to overall pass rate
v043_trap_evidence.interpretation: All 5 cohorts scored 0.87-0.91 — degenerate signal indicating the bun-test pass rate was driven by unrelated apps/api and apps/cli tests, not the chart UX directive.
